findings.title: No code for OrthoTrans/NormLin plug-in experiments (iTransformer, PatchTST, RLinear)
findings.quote:
self.model_dict = {
    'OLinear': OLinear,
    'OLinear_C': OLinear_C,
    'OLinear_attn_var': OLinear_attn_var,
    'OLinear_ablation_var_temp': OLinear_ablation_var_temp,
    'OLinear_ablation_lin_design': OLinear_ablation_lin_design,
    'OLinear_no_Q_neither': OLinear_no_Q_neither,
    'OLinear_FFT': OLinear_FFT,
    'OLinear_wavelet': OLinear_wavelet,
    'OLinear_wavelet2': OLinear_wavelet2,
    'OLinear_Legendre': OLinear_Legendre,
    'OLinear_Laguerre': OLinear_Laguerre,
    'OLinear_cheby': OLinear_cheby,
}
findings.claim: The model registry contains only OLinear and its own variants/ablations; no iTransformer / PatchTST / RLinear / DLinear / TimeMixer model is registered or present in model/, although run.py defines flags iTrans_ortho_trans, PatchTST_ortho_trans, DLinear_ortho_trans (run.py:78-80).
findings.concern: One of the paper's three headline contributions — that OrthoTrans/NormLin as a plug-in 'consistently improves the performance of existing forecasters' (Table 5, Tables 9-10) — has no runnable code in the repo, so the generality results cannot be reproduced.
findings.resolution: Provide the modified baseline models (iTransformer/PatchTST/RLinear with OrthoTrans and NormLin plug-ins) and their scripts, or point to where they live.
findings.paper_ref: Section 5.2 'OrthoTrans as a plug-in', Table 5; Tables 9-10
findings.reason: exp_basic.py:13-25 matches the quote exactly; model/ contains only OLinear variants with no iTransformer/PatchTST/RLinear implementations, and run.py confirms the plugin flags exist but no corresponding models are registered.

findings.title: Channel-correlation matrices required by OLinear-C absent for all bundled datasets except weather
findings.claim: OLinear-C (model/OLinear_C.py:42-45) requires a per-dataset channel-correlation matrix `*_COV_channel_ratio0.70.npy`, but of the 50 script references to bundled datasets only weather's file exists; 44/50 referenced channel files are absent (cars, covid, DowJones, ILI, nasdaq, power, SP500, unemployment, website, wiki).
findings.concern: Table 7 (OLinear-C results) and the var-corr / attn-var ablations cannot be reproduced out-of-the-box on the bundled datasets, because the channel matrices the scripts load are not shipped (they must be regenerated via dataset/Generate_corrmat.ipynb, which the README presents as optional).
findings.resolution: Ship the `*_COV_channel_ratio0.70.npy` files for all bundled datasets, or state in the README that Generate_corrmat.ipynb must be run first to produce them.
findings.reason: cars_orthoLinear.sh:35-39 matches the quote exactly, confirming the script references a channel file that must be pre-generated; the OLinear_C.py code at lines 42-47 also confirms the file is required at runtime.

findings.title: None of the 11 baseline models are present in the repo
findings.quote:
We carefully choose 11 well-acknowledged state-of-the-art forecasting models as our
baselines
findings.claim: The paper compares OLinear against 11 baselines (Tables 2, 3, 8), but model/ contains no baseline implementation (checked: iTransformer, PatchTST, DLinear, TimeMixer, Leddam, CARD, Fredformer, FITS, FilterNet, TimesNet all ABSENT); only OLinear variants are registered.
findings.concern: Baseline numbers cannot be reproduced from this repo and there is no script that runs baselines under the same split/metric/tuning budget, so the head-to-head comparison is not independently checkable here.
findings.resolution: Either include the baseline code/scripts used to produce Tables 2/3/8, or cite the exact source (repo+commit) and configuration for each baseline number.
findings.reason: paper_text.txt line 624 confirms the exact quote; model/ contains only OLinear variants (8 files) with no iTransformer, PatchTST, DLinear, TimeMixer or other baseline implementations.

findings.title: requirements.txt lists no versions; 'pywt' is not an installable PyPI name
findings.quote:
pandas
scikit-learn
numpy
matplotlib
torch
fvcore
einops
thop
timm
reformer_pytorch
openpyxl
seaborn
pywt
findings.claim: All dependencies are unpinned (no versions), and the final entry `pywt` is not a valid PyPI distribution name (the package is `PyWavelets`, import name `pywt`); `pip install -r requirements.txt` will fail on `pywt`.
findings.concern: The exact environment cannot be rebuilt from the spec, and a literal `pip install -r requirements.txt` errors out on `pywt`, which is used by the wavelet-basis ablation (model/orthoLinear_basis/OLinear_wavelet.py:10).
findings.resolution: Pin versions (especially torch/numpy) and replace `pywt` with `PyWavelets` (or `pywavelets`).
findings.reason: requirements.txt matches the quote exactly — 13 unpinned entries, final entry is `pywt` which is not a valid PyPI package name (correct name is PyWavelets).

findings.title: Basis-ablation scripts request model keys 'OLinear_wavelet_concat'/'OLinear_wavelet2_concat' not in model_dict
findings.quote: model_names=(OLinear_FFT OLinear_wavelet_concat OLinear_wavelet2 OLinear_cheby OLinear_Laguerre OLinear_Legendre)
findings.claim: The active model_names arrays in scripts/ablation/basis/{ECL,PEMS03,Solar}_*basis.sh include `OLinear_wavelet_concat` (and `OLinear_wavelet2_concat` in PEMS03/Solar), but model_dict (experiments/exp_basic.py:13-25) only defines `OLinear_wavelet`/`OLinear_wavelet2`; exp_forecast.py:218 indexes `self.model_dict[self.args.model]` with no default.
findings.concern: Running these basis-ablation scripts as shipped raises `KeyError: 'OLinear_wavelet_concat'` at model construction, so the wavelet rows of Table 4 cannot be produced via these scripts.
findings.resolution: Rename the model_names entries to `OLinear_wavelet`/`OLinear_wavelet2`, or add the `*_concat` variants to model_dict.
findings.reason: ECL_orthoLinear_basis.sh line 6 matches the quote exactly with `OLinear_wavelet_concat` in active model_names; exp_basic.py:13-25 confirms only `OLinear_wavelet`/`OLinear_wavelet2` are registered, so the key mismatch is real.

findings.title: OLinear-C guard for missing channel file uses `assert ValueError(...)` (always truthy, never raises)
findings.claim: When the channel-correlation file is absent, the intended error path is `assert ValueError(...)`, which asserts the truthiness of a (truthy) exception object and therefore never raises; `self.channel_corr_mat` is left unset and the code later fails with an opaque AttributeError at OLinear_C.py:74.
findings.concern: The defensive check does not do what it intends (it should `raise ValueError`), so a missing channel matrix produces a confusing downstream crash instead of the intended clear message — compounding the missing-file issue (see channel-corr-matrices-missing).
findings.reason: OLinear_C.py:42-47 matches the quote exactly; line 47 confirms `assert ValueError(...)` which creates but does not raise the exception, leaving self.channel_corr_mat unset.
